name: Umbilical Hernia
description: An umbilical hernia is a defect in the abdominal wall at the umbilicus (belly button) through which abdominal fat or, less commonly, intestine protrudes. Small hernias often resolve spontaneously; large ones require surgical repair.
symptoms: Visible soft swelling at belly button
Usually reducible (can be pushed back in)
May become hard or painful if intestine is trapped (emergency)
Generally no other signs in simple cases
treatment_overview: Small hernias (under 1 cm) often resolve by 6 months. Larger or persistent hernias are repaired surgically, often at the time of neutering. Emergency surgery is needed if strangulation occurs.
prevention_tips: Do not breed from dogs with known hereditary umbilical hernia tendency
